events.title: Altered States Exhibition and Experience
events.address: Room 235B, Student Union Building  41 Campus Center Way  Amherst, MA 01003
events.description: Altered States is an exhibition and a full weekend of experience. Two artists. Two visual languages. One source. Tyler Wcislo's digital collages erupt with color, sacred geometry, and the raw exposure of interior states. Ross Gronvold's sculptural works pull toward elemental silence — deeply textural, form arrived at through material intelligence. Both speaking from somewhere beyond the self and asking you to listen. FRI APR 17, 6–8PM Opening Reception + Live Music w/ Father Otter SAT APR 18, 6–9PM Live Music w/ Draiodoir + open floor SUN APR 19, 5–7:30PMContemplative Movement + Live Sound Stu

events.title: Pack the Pantry Food Drive! April 20-29
events.description: Help Pack the ASC@UMass: The Campus Pantry by donating non-perishable foods at these collection bin locations. The Campus Pantry, located at 472 North Pleasant Street (lower level), provides free groceries to support the well-being of UMass students, faculty, staff, and their families. No donation is too small. When: April 20-29Where: Various locations, April 29 - Haigis Mall NorthDonate: Non-perishable items Co-hosted by the UMass Amherst Community Campaign (UMACC), Basic Needs Initiatives, and Recreation and Wellbeing (RecWell), Pack the Pantry runs April 20–29 and is gathering non-perishabl

events.title: Drawing Curious Spaces - Natural History Exhibit
events.description: The Drawing Curious Spaces Series is comprised of a number of informal gatherings at some quirky and less know spaces on campus focused on slow seeing and drawing. No registration necessary; drop-in style means you come for what you can. Each gathering is a stand-alone event. No drawing experience necessary; each attendee gets use of a drawing kit. This project is funded by the Office of Equity and Inclusion.

events.title: Workshop: Support your Community
events.description: Prepare to be an active bystander when you see a situation that doesn’t feel right. April is Sexual Assault Awareness Month (SAAM).

events.title: Defining and Identifying Antisemitism workshop for undergraduates
events.description: As part of our Multifaith UMass initiative, undergraduate students are invited to two introductory workshops on antisemitism and Islamophobia. Registration is encouraged, but not required, to attend the sessions. Defining and Identifying Islamophobia April 14, 2026, 6:00 - 7:30 pm, Goodell Hall 329 Defining and Identifying Antisemitism April 21, 2026, 6:00 - 7:30 pm, Goodell Hall 329 The sessions are facilitated by Brad Seligmann, Director of the Office of Religious and Spiritual Life.

events.title: Libraries' Page to Screen Club
events.description: Drop in to the Libraries' multimedia book club, where we read a book and then watch the screen adaptation! We meet for several weeks to talk about the book, then watch the screen adaptation together and compare. All sessions are 7-8:30 p.m. except for screenings, which are 7-9:30 p.m. The breakdown for what pages/sections to read will be determined at a later planning session. Little Women, Louisa May Alcott February 3 February 10 February 17 February 24 March 3 - Screening Stories of Your Life and Others, Ted Chiang March 10 March 17 - No meeting; spring vacation March 24 - Screening Jurassic

events.title: 2026 Wellness Fair
events.description: The UMass Amherst Public Health Promotion Center, in collaboration with the Elaine Marieb College of Nursing and the School of Public Health & Health Sciences. will host its fourth annual Wellness Fair on Earth Day, Wednesday, April 22 from 11 a.m – 2 p.m. in the Campus Center Auditorium. The free, day-long event is open to all UMass Amherst students, faculty, and staff. The 2026 Wellness Fair strives to educate and celebrate the ongoing efforts to promote integrated approaches to wellness in support of being a health-promoting university through the Okanagan Charter and the Okanagan Wellbeing

events.title: Earth Day Celebration with UMass Dining Sustainability
events.description: Celebrate Earth Day with us! Join us on Wednesday April 22nd from 11 AM–2 PM at the Campus Center for a day of sustainable eats and inspiration. Stop by Harvest Market for a featured climate-friendly menu, enjoy free tastings, grab some giveaways, and explore simple ways to make more climate-conscious choices. Then keep the momentum going, a climate-friendly menu options will be available at all dining commons from 5–9 PM. Enjoy great food, discover new favorites, and see how small actions can add up to a big impact!

events.title: Plants, Pots & Painting
events.description: Take a creative break with USSC at our Plants, Pots & Painting Event! Join us for a relaxing afternoon where you can paint a personalized home for a new succulent while connecting with fellow first-generation students. All supplies, including pots and plants, will be provided! Just bring your creativity and a friend. This is a perfect opportunity to unwind, meet new people, and leave with something green and uniquely yours! Families are welcome! Feel free to bring your children along for the fun. Please let us know you’re coming by registering at this link!

events.title: Wellbeing Wednesday: HeART Work
events.description: Explore how both art making and music can support and boost your mental, physical, and social wellbeing. Enjoy live music that helps set a welcoming, creative atmosphere from 5:30-6pm, then join us in co-creating a community arts mural with UMass Creative Arts Therapist Cathe Bruso, LMHC, from 6-7pm. Presented in partnership by UMass Downtown and UMass Recwell, Wellbeing Wednesday @ UMass Downtown events are free and open to all.

events.title: Wellness Paint n' Sip
events.description: Relax. Recharge. Enjoy. You are invited each month to a cozy evening of painting, wellness, and tasty treats. Snacks and supplies are provided. Every fourth Wednesday (dates: 2/25, 3/25, 4/22,*except 5/13*) from 5:30-7:30PM in the Theater Room of New Africa House building. You may register here: https://forms.gle/iTnc9g2JDKMdU3xw7 . RSVPs preferred, drop ins are welcome! This series is a Campus Climate Improvement Grant (CCIG) sponsored event, open to any students, staff & faculty.

events.title: SEL Science Fun Nights: Window Clings to Protect Birds
events.description: Drop in to create colorful window clings with paint and glue! Stick them to your windows at home to prevent bird strikes. Designs provided or design your own. They will need time to dry so plan to pick them up on Friday 4/24.
